title: Review Gemini OAuth subscription — antigravity key bans
description: Gemini provider uses antigravity/internal OAuth key that is getting users banned. Gemini CLI uses its own OAuth flow (user authenticates with their own Google account). Need to: 1) investigate how Gemini CLI does OAuth (likely ~/.config/gcloud or similar), 2) decide if we should implement the same flow, 3) check if current antigravity key is actually our key or user-provided. Risky: using an internal key for production users is a TOS violation.

logs.msg: Research complete. Summary: ion already uses Gemini CLI client ID (681255809395-...) not Antigravity. BUT both are causing bans. Active ban wave Feb 2026: OpenClaw/opencode users getting accounts permanently banned for using either Antigravity OR Gemini CLI OAuth in third-party tools. Error: 'Gemini has been disabled in this account for violation of Terms of Service.' Bans are silent/immediate with no warning. Paid users (/mo) affected. Community recommends: use official API key or BYO OAuth credentials from own GCP project. Options for ion: (1) remove Google OAuth subscription support, (2) add ban warning, (3) require BYOK for OAuth credentials.
